speaker: Luis de Guindos
tone_summary: De Guindos' single readable speech in the window — the Ragnar Nurkse Memorial Lecture in Tallinn — is framed around turbulence and resilience rather than monetary policy direction. He acknowledges strong 2025 euro area growth (1.5%) and falling inflation, which sets a benign backdrop, but flags deep geopolitical uncertainty from US trade policy shifts and the erosion of the multilateral order. His tone is concerned but not alarmist, and he does not advocate for any specific policy direction. With only one speech available and no explicit rate commentary, he scores near neutral.
representative_quote: A new paradigm has emerged, where the rule of law is challenged by the rule of power.
